L1.area: Recipe execution and step card rendering pipeline

L2.description: A cross-site scripting vulnerability exists where user-controlled content from an untrusted recipe is rendered through template variables without proper escaping. When a recipe is executed in a notebook environment, attacker-controlled data is interpolated into HTML output, enabling arbitrary script injection and client-side remote code execution.

L3.area: Recipe step card rendering and data ingestion card generation
L3.description: A cross-site scripting vulnerability exists where user-controlled content from an untrusted recipe is rendered through Jinja2 template variables without applying the escape filter. When step cards are built during recipe execution in a Jupyter Notebook environment, attacker-controlled data such as error tracebacks and dataset schema information is interpolated into HTML output without sanitization, enabling arbitrary script injection and client-side remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: Insufficient sanitization of Jinja2 template variables in MLflow recipe step cards allows attacker-controlled content to be rendered as raw HTML, enabling cross-site scripting and client-side remote code execution when running untrusted recipes in Jupyter Notebook.
